message:
Migrate all connector tests to fcp_async_core::runtime::test and remove redundant tokio dev-deps

Systematic migration across all 60+ connectors:

Test runtime migration:
  - Replace #[tokio::test] with #[fcp_async_core::runtime::test] in
    every connector's integration test suite, aligning with the new
    runtime-agnostic async test macro from fcp-async-core-macros
  - This enables future runtime flexibility without per-connector changes

Cargo.toml cleanup:
  - Remove tokio = { workspace = true, features = ["macros", "rt-multi-thread"] }
    from dev-dependencies across all connectors — the test runtime is now
    provided transitively through fcp-async-core's macro re-export

annas-archive connector fixes:
  - connector.rs: Implementation adjustments
  - error.rs: Error type refinements
  - types.rs: Type definition updates

Affected connectors (60+): 1password, airtable, algolia, amplitude,
annas-archive, anthropic, arxiv, asana, bigquery, bitbucket, bitwarden,
box, browser, clickup, cron, datadog, discord, docusign, dropbox, duckdb,
elasticsearch, evernote, figma, github, gitlab, gmail, google-ai,
google-calendar, grafana, homeassistant, hubspot, intercom, jira,
kubernetes, linear, linkedin, llm-router, logseq, mailchimp, make,
mcp-bridge, metabase, microsoft365, mixpanel, monday, mongodb, n8n,
notion, openai, pandadoc, pinecone, plaid, posthog, pulumi, qdrant,
reddit, retool, roam, s3, salesforce, segment, semanticscholar,
sendgrid, sentry, slack, snowflake, spotify, stripe, telegram,
terraform, todoist, trello, twilio, twitter, vectordb, webhook-receiver,
youtube, zapier, zendesk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
